prose.overview: The Vintage Club carries a distinction shared by only a handful of Thai courses: it was designed by a single internationally renowned architect whose sole Thailand commission this remains. American architect Arthur Hills — responsible for courses including Treetops Resort, Stonebridge, and dozens of US designs — created The Vintage Club in Samut Prakan in 1996, blending his characteristic emphasis on naturalistic bunkering, strategic par-4 routing, and landscape preservation. The result is a course that retains marshes, reeds, and seasonal flora that soften the playing corridors and create a genuine countryside feel despite being 48 km from central Bangkok. The Tudor English-style clubhouse adds a unique visual character, and the on-site Japanese restaurant has a strong local following. The 350-yard grass driving range is one of the better warm-up facilities in the Bangkok south corridor.
prose.layout_and_experience: Hills' design philosophy at The Vintage Club prioritises visual beauty and strategic variety over pure difficulty. The course stretches 6,952 yards from the back tees across gently rolling terrain, with marsh, reed beds, and multi-coloured bougainvillea framing many fairways. Water comes into play on numerous holes but is generally placed to frame landing zones rather than force high-risk carries, making the course accessible to mid and high handicappers while still offering genuine challenge from the back tees. The par-3 2nd, par-5 10th, and par-4 13th are frequently cited as signature holes. Bunkering follows Hills' naturalistic style — irregular shapes and placement that look organic rather than artificial. Greens roll at a medium pace with mild undulation, favouring a standard approach game. The overall experience rewards accuracy over length and repays multiple visits with new strategic discoveries. Caddies are well regarded for reading the greens and providing yardage guidance. Non-metal spikes are required. Weekend rounds carry a 500 THB surcharge for non-playing followers.
prose.tips: The Vintage Club is a solid mid-range choice in the Bangkok south corridor — green fees approximately 2,100 THB weekday and 2,600 THB weekend, plus cart (750 THB) and caddie fees. Budget around 3,500–4,000 THB all-in for a weekend round. Book at least 2–3 days in advance for weekdays and a week ahead for popular weekend slots. The grass driving range is one of the best in the area — arrive 30 minutes early. Non-metal spikes are compulsory; the pro shop sells replacements if needed. Contact: +66 2 707 3820. Located 48 km south-east of Bangkok near the Bangna-Trad Highway, the drive from Sukhumvit takes 50 minutes off-peak. Club hire at 1,500 THB.
prose.location_and_access: The Vintage Club is located at 549, 549/1, 4 Moo 3, Khlong Dan, Bang Bo District, Samut Prakan 10550, approximately 48 km south-east of central Bangkok. The drive from Sukhumvit takes 50 minutes via Motorway 7 (Bangna-Trad Expressway). Suvarnabhumi International Airport is approximately 20 km away. Taxis and Grab cars service the route. The surrounding area is quiet rural Samut Prakan, with some local restaurants near Bang Bo.
prose.rental_cta_context: The Vintage Club is 50 minutes from Bangkok's hotel district — LENGOLF delivers tour-quality rental clubs to your door the night before, so you arrive ready to play Arthur Hills' only Thailand design.

en.title: The Vintage Club — Green Fees, Course Guide & Golf Club Rentals
en.meta_description: The Vintage Club Bangkok green fees, Arthur Hills course overview, tips for visiting, and how to arrange golf club rentals delivered to your Bangkok hotel.

verification_notes: Designer Arthur Hills confirmed by multiple sources. GPS confirmed by where2golf.com. Green fees 2,100/2,600 from golfsavers bracket. Cart 750 confirmed by golfasian.com. Weekday/weekend split is interpreted from price bracket.
disputes: Year opened: golfbangkok 1996 vs where2golf 1995. Using 1996.
Weekend fee interpretation from golfsavers price range 2,100-2,600.
